level1: Fisherfolk whisper that the sea glows faintly at night since the quake; those who sail into it sometimes don't return.
A massive oak split open during the tremor, revealing a hollow large enough to crawl into—something inside breathes at night.
A shallow cave appeared near the cliffs; inside are crude symbols scratched into the walls, still wet.
A local hunter swears he saw a deer with two heads fleeing deeper into the forest after the earthquake.
Pieces of black stone sometimes wash ashore now—warm to the touch, even in cold water.
Seabirds refuse to land in a particular stretch of coast, circling endlessly above it.
An old well in a ruined cottage has started filling with saltwater instead of fresh.
A traveling peddler insists there's a new path in the woods that wasn't there before—and it always leads you back to the same tree.
Strange footprints, too large for any man, have been found near fishing huts after stormy nights.
The roots of several trees have been pushed above ground, forming natural tunnels—something small and quick darts between them.
A patch of forest has gone silent; no insects, no birds, just stillness.
A group of goblins has been spotted arguing loudly over something shiny they pulled from a crack in the earth.
Villagers gossip that the earthquake woke something old beneath the cliffs, and it's hungry.
A half-mad delver keeps raving about a sealed stair beneath a sunken crypt—says the stone door is etched with gold inlay. He couldn’t open it… but he’s certain it leads to untouched vaults below.
A fisherman claims to have caught a fish with human-like eyes, and it whispered something in an unknown language before dying.
A drunk grave digger says the marsh is ‘spitting the dead back out.’ Coffins are rising to the surface after the rains… and if the dead are coming up, there must be something below pushing them.
A tinker passing through town is paying good coin for old coins stamped with unfamiliar kings—says they only turn up in the marsh barrows. Won’t say why he wants them… just that he needs more.
A band of treasure hunters returned wealthy—but tight-lipped. The only clue: their gear is marked with fresh dwarven maker’s marks, not ancient ones.
A distant cousin of Berntal wrote begging for help—then sends a second letter denying everything, claiming nothing is wrong at all.
level2: A collapsed section of cliff has revealed ancient stone stairs descending into darkness, carved with sea motifs.
A band of smugglers has taken over a newly formed sea cave, but they've stopped coming back out.
A twisted treant has begun wandering the forest edge, its bark cracked and bleeding sap like ichor.
The ruins of an old watchtower have resurfaced inland, as if pushed up from beneath the soil.
Fishermen report a massive shadow moving beneath their boats, pacing them just below the surface.
A druid circle has been disturbed; the stones are cracked, and something has clawed deep gouges into them.
A burrow network has appeared overnight—occupied by pale, blind creatures that hunt in packs.
An abandoned shrine near the coast now hums faintly, and anyone who sleeps nearby dreams of drowning.
A merchant caravan vanished on a forest road, leaving behind only splintered wagons and deep furrows in the ground.
A giant boar, driven mad by the quake, rampages through the woods, its tusks chipped with stone.
A fisherman found a sealed bottle washed ashore, containing a map to a hidden underwater cave system.
A cracked milestone was found half-buried in the hills, marked with ancient dwarven runes pointing toward a ‘Deep Hall of Kings.’ Scholars are paying for escorts to investigate.
A soldier deserted his post after claiming he saw the same man in Ragged Hollow wearing many different faces.
level3: A fissure beneath the forest leads to a flooded cavern system inhabited by something ancient and amphibious.
A ruined lighthouse offshore has begun shining again, though no one has lived there for decades.
A sect of cultists has gathered around a newly revealed monolith, attempting to awaken whatever lies beneath.
The earthquake uncovered a buried ship deep inland, its hold still intact—and guarded by restless dead.
A colossal serpent has been sighted along the coastline, possibly displaced from its lair by the tremor.
A dying historian insisted that Dwarrowdeep was never abandoned—only sealed. And seals, he said, are meant to be broken… eventually.
The baron of Swordshade is paying for anyone willing to verify a rumor that an entire village appeared overnight decades ago—and hasn’t changed since.
